mechanism: Fiscal dominance erodes long-run monetary credibility, driving non-Western central banks to accumulate gold as a reserve diversification hedge. The $400-600/oz residual above real-rate-implied gold prices partly reflects this structural credibility loss, though post-Russia sanctions reserve diversification is a competing explanation.
regime_dependence: Holds under conditions of persistent fiscal dominance and geopolitical sanctions risk. Competing explanation (post-Russia sanctions diversification) may dominate in the short term. Dollar reserve share decline (59% to 57%) is glacial.
